mcpConnections.title: MCP Connections
mcpConnections.description: Connect AI apps securely through MCP protocol

toasts.clientRedirectRequired: Add at least one redirect URI
toasts.tokenCreated: Token created — copy it now. You won't see it again.

connectSection.title: Connect an AI app to your account
connectSection.description: This page helps you connect AI apps to your workspace. Start by copying the app address below into the app you want to connect. Then choose one of the two supported sign-in methods.

oauthMethod.title: OAuth 2.1 connector
oauthMethod.description: Use this when the app can open a browser for sign-in. You sign in, approve access, and the app connects automatically. This is the best option for most users.

tokenMethod.title: Access token
tokenMethod.description: Use this only when the app asks you to paste a token, API key, or secret. You create the token here and paste it into the app yourself.

appAddress.label: App address
appAddress.instruction: Copy this into the AI app when it asks where to connect.

advancedUrl.label: Advanced sign-in URL
advancedUrl.instruction: Only use this if the app specifically asks for a sign-in or discovery URL.

1.title: 1. Copy Address
1.desc: Paste it into the AI app to start setup.

2.title: 2. Choose Method
2.desc: Use OAuth if it opens a browser, otherwise use a token.

3.title: 3. Manage Here
3.desc: View connections or revoke access anytime.

jsonSetup.title: Need to use a JSON configuration?
jsonSetup.subtitle: Click to show or hide JSON setup blocks
jsonSetup.instruction: Some apps ask you to paste a JSON setup block instead of filling out a form. Use the OAuth 2.1 version when the app supports browser sign-in. Use the access token version when the app asks for a token.
jsonSetup.tokenInstruction: Replace YOUR_MCP_TOKEN with a token from this page, then paste the finished JSON into the app.

tokens.title: Access Tokens
tokens.tip: Use an access token only if the app does not support the OAuth 2.1 connector. Keep tokens private, like a password. In most cases, you will paste the app address and this token into the app.

empty.title: No access tokens
empty.desc: Create a token to connect your first AI app manually.

oauth.title: OAuth 2.1 Connections
oauth.tip: Apps that you connect with the OAuth 2.1 connector will appear here automatically. After you approve access in the browser, the app will show up here.

edit.title: Edit callbacks for {{name}}
edit.description: Update the callback URLs used by this OAuth client. Keep one redirect URI per line.

form.redirectUrisHint: Enter one redirect URI per line. Redirect URIs must use HTTPS or localhost.
form.created: OAuth client created — copy the credentials now.
form.createdHint: The client secret is only shown once. Store it in the MCP app or secret manager before closing this panel.

empty.title: No OAuth clients connected
empty.desc: Apps will appear here after sign-in is completed in your AI app.

confirm.revokeClientMessage: Revoke all access tokens for "{{name}}"? The client will need to re-authenticate.
confirm.revokeTokenMessage: Revoke the token "{{name}}"? Any MCP client using this token will lose access immediately.

assistant.title: Assistant

greetings.0: What do you have in mind?
greetings.1: What do you want to create today?
greetings.2: How can I assist your workflow?
greetings.3: Ready to make some magic happen?
greetings.8: Welcome to the Desert of the Real.

assistant.confirmTool: Approve this tool in this session
assistant.toolApprovalEnabled: Future {{tool}} actions in this conversation will auto-approve for this session.
assistant.errorOccurred: An error occurred. You can try again.
assistant.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this conversation?
assistant.noProvider: Select a provider and model to start chatting
assistant.providerNotConfigured: No chat-capable providers configured. Add an OpenAI, Claude, or Google AI provider first.
assistant.emptyChat: Start a conversation with your assistant
assistant.micUnsupported: Microphone is not supported in this browser
